assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response explains that a single node going down breaks the entire slice, but it does not mention the specific GKE infrastructure detail that TPU slices are upgraded/recreated atomically in a single step rather than via rolling upgrades.

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states that if one slice fails due to maintenance, the other healthy slices will lose communication and hang/crash, requiring an all-or-nothing restart of the entire 4-slice topology.

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response notes that the training framework will likely fail to initialize unless all pods across all 4 slices restart simultaneously, clearly indicating an interruption to the training job.

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response does not mention anything about hardware capacity, insufficient TPU resources, or the possibility of the slice blocking and causing extended downtime during recreation.

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response advises increasing checkpoint frequency leading up to the maintenance window and catching the SIGTERM signal to trigger one final synchronous 'lifeboat' checkpoint.

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response recommends configuring the training script to 'auto-resume from the latest GCS checkpoint on startup' so it picks up exactly where it left off.

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response clearly suggests setting a 'Maintenance Exclusion' in GKE to block the update for up to 30 days if the training run is close to finishing.

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response recommends configuring a 'Maintenance Window' in GKE cluster settings to control the timing of the event to a period when the team is monitoring the system.
